title: US Launches Operation Burning Spear: Massive Airstrikes on IRGC Bases Across Iran
description: CENTCOM executes the opening salvo of Operation Burning Spear with over 200 sorties flown by B-2 Spirit stealth bombers, F-35A Lightning IIs, and carrier-based F/A-18 Super Hornets. Targets include IRGC command-and-control bunkers near Tehran, ballistic missile production facilities at Parchin, and Shahed drone factories in Isfahan. Tomahawk cruise missiles launched from USS Philippine Sea and USS Gettysburg in the Gulf of Oman. Initial BDA indicates significant destruction of IRGC Aerospace Force infrastructure. Iran vows massive retaliation.

title: Iran Fires 300+ Ballistic Missiles at US Bases in Gulf Region

location.name: NSA Bahrain & Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar

description: Iran retaliates with its largest-ever ballistic missile barrage: over 300 Emad, Sejjil-2, and Fattah-2 hypersonic missiles targeting US Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain, Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar, and Camp Arifjan in Kuwait. US THAAD and Patriot PAC-3 batteries intercept the majority but several impacts reported at NSA Bahrain. Multiple US casualties confirmed. Bahrain international airport suspends all flights. GCC nations invoke collective defense provisions.

title: Naval Battle of the Strait of Hormuz: IRGC Fast Boats Attack US Carrier Group
description: IRGC Navy launches coordinated assault with 40+ fast-attack boats, 6 explosive-laden unmanned surface vessels, and shore-based anti-ship cruise missiles against the USS Dwight D. Eisenhower carrier strike group transiting the Strait of Hormuz. USS Laboon and USS Mason destroy 28 fast boats and all USVs. One Noor anti-ship missile strikes USS Gravely (DDG-107), killing 12 sailors. Iran loses an estimated 200+ naval personnel. The Strait is temporarily closed to commercial traffic.

title: Israel Strikes Iranian Nuclear Facilities at Natanz and Fordow
description: Israeli Air Force executes long-planned strikes on Iranian nuclear infrastructure using F-35I Adir stealth fighters armed with GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators (loaned by US). Targets include underground enrichment halls at Natanz and the deeply buried Fordow facility. Multiple penetrating strikes on Fordow's mountain bunker. IAEA confirms significant damage to centrifuge cascades. Iran declares nuclear facilities destroyed constitute a 'declaration of war' by Israel. Russia and China condemn strikes at emergency UNSC session.

title: PMF Overruns US Forward Base Near Erbil, Iraq

location.name: K-1 Air Base, Kirkuk Province, Iraq

description: Iran-backed PMF forces, reinforced by IRGC Quds Force advisors, launch coordinated ground assault on US forward operating position near Erbil. Attack combines drone swarm, SVBIED, and infantry assault with 500+ fighters. US forces repel the assault with Apache helicopter gunships and AC-130 Spectre support but sustain significant casualties — 8 KIA, 31 WIA. Iraqi government demands US withdrawal, calling the presence 'destabilizing.' Kurdish Peshmerga provides perimeter security but does not engage PMF directly.

title: Houthi Anti-Ship Missile Sinks Greek Tanker in Red Sea
description: Houthi forces fire two Iranian-supplied Khalij Fars anti-ship ballistic missiles at the Greek-flagged VLCC MV Aegean Titan carrying 2 million barrels of crude oil. One missile strikes the engine room, causing catastrophic flooding. The vessel sinks within 6 hours, creating a massive oil spill in the Bab el-Mandeb strait. All 28 crew rescued by USS Carney. Brent crude jumps to $187/barrel. International shipping halts all Red Sea transits.

title: Iran Announces Closure of Strait of Hormuz to All Traffic
description: Supreme Leader Khamenei orders the closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all international shipping, declaring it an 'act of self-defense.' IRGC Navy begins deploying naval mines and positions anti-ship missile batteries along the coastline. Iran warns any vessel attempting transit will be treated as hostile. 21% of global oil supply at risk. Oil futures surge to $210/barrel. US declares the closure 'an act of war' and pledges to reopen the waterway by force. NATO activates Article 5 consultations.

title: CENTCOM Destroys Iranian Air Defense Network with SEAD Campaign
description: In the opening minutes of Operation Burning Spear, US Air Force F-22 Raptors and EA-18G Growlers execute Suppression of Enemy Air Defenses (SEAD) mission against Iran's Russian-supplied S-300PMU2 and domestically produced Bavar-373 air defense systems. AGM-88G AARGM-ER anti-radiation missiles destroy 14 of Iran's 29 known long-range SAM batteries. Cyber operations simultaneously disable Iran's integrated air defense command network. Iranian airspace effectively uncontested within 90 minutes.

title: Hezbollah Fires 3,000 Rockets at Northern Israel in 24 Hours
description: Hezbollah Secretary-General declares 'full solidarity' with Iran and unleashes the largest rocket barrage in Middle Eastern history. Over 3,000 rockets, including Fateh-110 and precision-guided Burkan missiles, target Haifa, the Galilee, and IDF military installations. Iron Dome intercepts estimated 75% but hundreds impact populated areas. Haifa port severely damaged. Israeli government authorizes full-scale ground operation into Lebanon. IDF begins massive aerial bombardment of Dahiyeh (Beirut southern suburbs). Civilian casualties mount on both sides.

title: US Cyber Command Disables Iranian Power Grid and Banking Systems
description: US Cyber Command executes Operation Digital Eclipse, a coordinated cyber campaign targeting Iran's civilian and military infrastructure. Attacks disable 60% of Iran's national power grid, bring down the Shetab banking network, disrupt GPS-dependent navigation for IRGC missile forces, and implant persistent access in IRGC command-and-control systems. Tehran experiences rolling blackouts. Iranian cyber forces attempt retaliatory attacks on US financial systems but are largely contained by NSA defenses.

title: Iran-Backed Militias Attack US Embassy Green Zone in Baghdad
description: Kata'ib Hezbollah and Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq fighters launch coordinated rocket and drone attack on the US Embassy compound in Baghdad's Green Zone. Over 40 rockets and 15 kamikaze drones target diplomatic facilities. C-RAM and counter-drone systems intercept most but embassy buildings sustain fire damage. Non-essential personnel evacuated by helicopter to Erbil. Marine Security Guards maintain perimeter. State Department orders all Americans to leave Iraq immediately.

title: THE TRIGGER: PMF Drone Swarm Kills 14 US Marines at Al-Asad Air Base

location.name: Ain al-Asad Air Base, Anbar Province, Iraq

description: The event that triggers the war. A coordinated swarm of 30+ Iranian-manufactured Shahed-136 kamikaze drones, launched from multiple positions across western Iraq, overwhelms C-RAM defenses at Al-Asad Air Base. Multiple drones impact Marine barracks and vehicle staging area. 14 US Marines killed, 45 wounded — the deadliest attack on US forces in the Middle East since the 1983 Beirut barracks bombing. Intelligence confirms IRGC Quds Force officers directly commanded the operation. President orders retaliatory strikes, initiating Operation Burning Spear.

title: UN Security Council Emergency Session: Russia and China Veto Ceasefire Resolution
description: Emergency UNSC session fails to produce a ceasefire resolution. Russia and China veto a Western-backed resolution demanding immediate cessation of hostilities and Iranian compliance with IAEA inspections. Russia introduces counter-resolution blaming US 'aggression.' China calls for 'restraint from all parties' while maintaining oil purchases from Iran through sanctions-evading channels. UN Secretary-General warns of 'catastrophic humanitarian consequences' and calls for urgent diplomatic corridor. Non-Aligned Movement countries demand emergency General Assembly session.

title: Oil Prices Shatter Records: Brent Crude Hits $210 as Markets Panic
description: Global oil markets enter freefall after Iran announces Strait of Hormuz closure. Brent crude futures surge past $210/barrel — shattering the 2008 record. S&P 500 drops 7%, triggering circuit breakers. European natural gas prices spike 40%. Strategic Petroleum Reserve releases announced by US, Japan, and South Korea. OPEC+ emergency meeting called for March 1. Economists warn of global recession within weeks if the strait remains closed. Gas prices in the US exceed $7/gallon.

title: US B-2 Bombers Strike IRGC Underground Missile Bunkers at Khorramabad
description: Three B-2 Spirit stealth bombers flying from Diego Garcia deliver GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators against deeply buried IRGC ballistic missile storage facilities in the Zagros Mountains near Khorramabad. The underground complex reportedly housed Shahab-3 and Emad medium-range ballistic missiles. Secondary explosions visible for hours suggest successful detonation of stored missile fuel and warheads. Iran claims the strikes hit civilian infrastructure.

title: Iranian Fattah-2 Hypersonic Missile Penetrates THAAD Defense at Al Udeid
description: In the second wave of Iranian missile retaliation, an IRGC Fattah-2 hypersonic glide vehicle successfully evades THAAD interceptors and strikes a hangar complex at Al Udeid Air Base. The HGV's maneuvering reentry profile defeats tracking algorithms. Two F-35As destroyed on the ground. 11 USAF personnel killed, 30+ wounded. The first confirmed kill by a hypersonic weapon in combat. Pentagon admits 'capability gap' in hypersonic defense. Congress demands emergency funding for Glide Phase Interceptor program.

title: IRGC Quds Force Commander Esmail Qaani Killed in Targeted Strike
description: US strike on IRGC headquarters complex in Tehran kills Brigadier General Esmail Qaani, commander of the Quds Force and successor to Qasem Soleimani. Intelligence confirms Qaani was in the building during the strike. His death decapitates Iran's proxy warfare command structure. Successor unclear — IRGC internal power struggle reported. Massive crowds flood Tehran streets in mourning. Supreme Leader Khamenei vows to 'erase the Zionist entity and its American patron from the map.'

title: Escalation Week: 4 US Bases Hit by PMF Drones in 48 Hours Before War
description: In the 48 hours following the Al-Asad massacre, PMF and Iranian-backed militias escalate with attacks on 4 additional US positions: Tower 22 in Jordan (8 rockets), Conoco gas field in Syria (drone swarm), Harir Air Base in Kurdistan (mortar barrage), and a logistics convoy near Taji. US retaliates with strikes on 7 PMF weapons depots and 3 safe houses. Pentagon announces deployment of additional carrier strike group (USS Gerald R. Ford) to the region.

title: President Addresses Nation: 'We Did Not Seek This War'
description: In a primetime address from the Oval Office, the President announces Operation Burning Spear, declaring the Al-Asad massacre was 'directed by the highest levels of the Iranian regime.' Outlines objectives: destroy Iran's ability to threaten US forces, degrade nuclear weapons capability, and neutralize proxy command networks. Explicitly states no ground invasion planned — 'This will not be another Iraq.' Congress receives War Powers notification. Authorization for Use of Military Force (AUMF) debate begins in Senate. Polls show 61% public support for strikes, but 78% oppose ground troops.

title: Iran Retaliatory Missile Strike on UAE and Saudi Oil Infrastructure
description: Iran expands the war beyond US targets, launching ballistic missiles at Saudi Aramco's Ras Tanura terminal and UAE's Jebel Ali port — the region's largest commercial port. THAAD batteries in UAE intercept 80% of incoming missiles but 3 impact the Ras Tanura facility, igniting storage tanks. Saudi Arabia and UAE formally declare war on Iran and commit air forces to the coalition. GCC activates Peninsula Shield Force. Oil output from Saudi Arabia drops by 3 million barrels/day.

title: IRGC Sleeper Cell Bombing in Dubai Mall — 43 Civilians Killed
description: An IRGC-directed sleeper cell detonates two vehicle-borne IEDs in the parking structure of the Dubai Mall during peak afternoon hours. The blasts collapse a section of the parking garage onto the ground floor retail area. 43 civilians killed including 11 children and 7 Western tourists. 200+ wounded. UAE security forces arrest 3 suspects within hours — all Iranian nationals linked to IRGC Quds Force intelligence network. Iran denies involvement but intercepted communications confirm Tehran authorization. The attack transforms global public opinion — previously neutral nations condemn Iran. Dubai's Burj Khalifa district placed under martial law. UAE vows to 'hunt down every Iranian agent on our soil.'

title: Iranian Ballistic Missile Hits Residential Area in Riyadh — 67 Dead

location.name: Al Olaya District, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia

description: An Iranian Emad ballistic missile targeting the Saudi Ministry of Defense headquarters in Riyadh misses by 2.4 kilometers and impacts a residential/commercial block in the Al Olaya financial district. A 12-story office building partially collapses. 67 civilians killed, 300+ injured. Children's hospital 400 meters from impact zone sustains significant blast damage. Saudi Arabia calls it 'Iran's war crime.' Iranian state media claims the target was military. UN Human Rights Council calls emergency session. The deadliest single missile strike of the conflict.

title: Iran-Backed Cell Attempts Car Bomb at US Embassy in Ankara, Turkey
description: Turkish intelligence (MIT) intercepts and neutralizes an IRGC-linked cell attempting to detonate a car bomb near the US Embassy in Ankara. Two operatives killed in shootout, one captured alive. The captured operative reveals a coordinated campaign to attack US and Israeli diplomatic facilities across 6 countries. Turkey, despite refusing to join the coalition, condemns Iran and recalls its ambassador from Tehran. Erdogan declares 'Iran has crossed every line.'

title: US Navy SEALs Capture Iranian Minelayer in Strait of Hormuz
description: Navy SEALs from SEAL Team 3 conduct a nighttime boarding operation against an IRGC Navy vessel caught deploying naval mines in the western approaches of the Strait of Hormuz. The vessel, a converted dhow carrying 40 EM-52 rocket-propelled mines, is captured intact with its crew of 8 IRGC sailors. The captured mines and navigation data provide intelligence on the full Iranian mine deployment pattern, allowing US MCM forces to prioritize clearance operations. Pentagon calls it a 'significant tactical breakthrough' for Hormuz reopening efforts.

title: Massive Anti-War Protests Erupt in Tehran Despite Blackouts
description: Despite rolling blackouts caused by US cyber attacks on the power grid, tens of thousands of Iranians take to the streets in Tehran, Isfahan, and Shiraz — not in support of the regime, but demanding an end to the war. Chants of 'Not our war!' and 'Death to the dictator!' echo through darkened streets. IRGC Basij militia deploys to suppress protests, firing rubber bullets and tear gas. Social media (via Starlink satellite internet) shows clashes between protesters and security forces. At least 12 protesters killed. The protests suggest internal Iranian opposition to the war that could influence regime decision-making.

title: China Deploys PLA Navy Destroyer Group to Gulf of Oman — 'Escort Operations'
description: China announces deployment of a Type 055 Renhai-class destroyer and two Type 054A frigates to the Gulf of Oman for 'escort and protection of Chinese-flagged commercial vessels.' The move places PLA Navy warships in close proximity to the US carrier strike groups. Pentagon calls the deployment 'unhelpful.' Beijing warns the US against 'any interference with Chinese maritime interests.' Chinese-flagged tankers continue attempting to purchase Iranian oil via sanctioned ship-to-ship transfers. Russia announces it will send a Pacific Fleet detachment 'in solidarity.' The conflict risks great power entanglement.

title: IDF Ground Forces Cross into Southern Lebanon — Full Invasion Begins
description: IDF 91st Division and 36th Armored Division cross the Lebanese border with 30,000+ troops, supported by massive artillery barrages and close air support. Objective: create a 30km buffer zone and destroy Hezbollah's Radwan Force tunnel network. Hezbollah fights fierce delaying actions with ATGMs (Kornets) destroying 4 Merkava IV tanks in the first hours. Intense urban combat in the town of Marjayoun. UNIFIL peacekeepers sheltering in hardened positions refuse to evacuate. Lebanon's government — powerless to stop either side — appeals to the UN for help. Civilian exodus from southern Lebanon overwhelms roads northward.

title: Iranian Cyber Attack Briefly Disrupts US East Coast Power Grid
description: Iranian APT group Charming Kitten activates pre-positioned malware in US East Coast power grid control systems, causing a 47-minute power disruption affecting 2.3 million customers across New Jersey, parts of New York, and Connecticut. CISA and NSA contain the attack and restore power. The malware — a variant of Industroyer2 — had been implanted months earlier via compromised vendor supply chain. DHS raises the Homeland Security Advisory System to 'Severe.' The attack demonstrates Iran can strike the US homeland through cyber means, raising the stakes of the conflict.
